channel.title: Math on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Math on RadixArk Blog

item.title: The Math Behind Attention Mechanisms
item.description:
Attention is all you need, but what exactly is attention mathematically?

In modern neural networks, the core mechanism allows a model to weigh the importance of different words in a sequence when processing a specific word.

The Scaled Dot-Product Attention

The fundamental equation that popularized the Transformer architecture is known as Scaled Dot-Product Attention. It is computed as follows:

$$ \text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\left(\frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V $$

Where:

  • \( Q \) is the query matrix.
  • \( K \) is the key matrix.
  • \( V \) is the value matrix.
  • \( d_k \) is the dimension of the keys.

The scaling factor \( \frac{1}{\sqrt{d_k}} \) is critical. Without it, the dot products grow extremely large in magnitude, pushing the softmax function into regions where it has extremely small gradients, leading to the vanishing gradient problem.
